one_liner: A 24-year-old male smoker presents with hemoptysis, rapidly progressive renal failure, and a nephritic urinary sediment over 1 week.

stage2.hpi_extension: The patient presented to the ED after 5 days of worsening hemoptysis, producing blood-tinged to frankly bloody sputum. He has had progressively decreasing urine output and noticed his urine is dark and 'tea-colored.' He reports fatigue, myalgias, and low-grade fevers. He has no prior history of kidney or lung disease. He smokes one pack of cigarettes daily and works as a house painter. He was recently exposed to hydrocarbon-based solvents at work. He denies illicit drug use, recent travel, or sick contacts.
stage2.family_history: No family history of kidney disease or autoimmune disease.
stage2.social_history: House painter. One pack/day smoker (6 pack-year history). No alcohol. No illicit drugs. Single, lives alone. Recent exposure to paint solvents and thinners.
stage2.physical_exam: Young male appearing acutely ill and pale. Mild respiratory distress. Conjunctival pallor. Oropharynx with blood-tinged secretions. Heart: tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs. Lungs: **diffuse bilateral crackles with scattered rhonchi**. Abdomen: soft, non-tender, no organomegaly. Extremities: no edema; No rash, purpura, or joint swelling; No nasal crusting or saddle nose deformity.

lab_results.clinical_significance: Rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis (RPGN) — creatinine rising from normal to severely elevated in days to weeks
Severely elevated, consistent with rapidly progressive renal failure

lab_results.result: 3+ blood, 2+ protein, >100 RBCs/hpf (many dysmorphic), abundant RBC casts, no WBC casts
lab_results.clinical_significance: Active nephritic sediment with RBC casts pathognomonic for glomerulonephritis
diagnostic of the suspected condition; antibodies target the NC1 domain of the alpha-3 chain of type IV collagen
Excludes concurrent ANCA-associated vasculitis (dual positivity occurs in ~30% of anti-GBM cases)
Severe anemia from pulmonary hemorrhage and blood loss
Normal; this condition does not consume complement

lab_results.result: pH 7.28, pCO2 28, pO2 62, HCO3 14 on room air
lab_results.clinical_significance: Metabolic acidosis with respiratory compensation; hypoxemia from diffuse alveolar hemorrhage
Paradoxically elevated DLCO is a hallmark of diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (carbon monoxide is absorbed by intra-alveolar blood)

imaging_results.result: Bilateral diffuse alveolar infiltrates sparing the apices and costophrenic angles, with air bronchograms. No pleural effusion.
imaging_results.clinical_significance: Classic pattern of diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (DAH); bilateral perihilar predominance

imaging_results.result: Light microscopy: crescentic glomerulonephritis with crescents in 85% of glomeruli. Immunofluorescence: linear IgG staining along the glomerular basement membrane. Electron microscopy: no immune deposits; GBM shows disruption.
imaging_results.clinical_significance: Linear IgG deposition along GBM is pathognomonic for this condition; extensive crescent formation indicates severe disease with poor renal prognosis

stage5.final_clue: The combination of pulmonary hemorrhage (hemoptysis, diffuse alveolar infiltrates, elevated DLCO) and rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis (RPGN with RBC casts), with positive anti-GBM.

debrief.classic_presentation: A young male smoker presents with the pulmonary-renal syndrome: hemoptysis from diffuse alveolar hemorrhage combined with rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis. Labs show anti-GBM antibodies, and renal biopsy reveals crescentic GN with linear IgG staining on immunofluorescence.
debrief.attending_mode_notes: Anti-GBM disease and ANCA vasculitis can coexist ('double-positive') in ~30% of anti-GBM cases, typically MPO-ANCA. These patients may have a slightly better renal prognosis than pure anti-GBM disease. The degree of crescent formation on biopsy is the strongest predictor of renal outcome: >80% crescents with dialysis dependence at presentation predicts <10% chance of renal recovery. Pulmonary hemorrhage can be fatal; smoking and hydrocarbon exposure increase risk by damaging the alveolar-capillary basement membrane, exposing the alpha-3(IV) collagen epitope. Unlike ANCA vasculitis, anti-GBM disease rarely recurs after antibodies are cleared.
debrief.pathophysiology: Anti-GBM antibodies (IgG) target the NC1 domain of the alpha-3 chain of type IV collagen, which is highly expressed in the glomerular and alveolar basement membranes. Antibody binding activates complement and triggers a necrotizing crescentic glomerulonephritis. Pulmonary involvement (Goodpasture syndrome) requires a 'second hit' that disrupts the alveolar endothelial barrier (smoking, infections, hydrocarbon exposure), exposing the normally sequestered alpha-3(IV) collagen to circulating antibodies. This produces diffuse alveolar hemorrhage. The disease is rare (~1 per million/year) and has a bimodal age distribution (20-30 and 60-70 years).
debrief.treatment_overview: Medical EMERGENCY — treatment must begin immediately. (1) Plasmapheresis (plasma exchange daily for 14 days or until anti-GBM antibodies are undetectable) to rapidly remove circulating anti-GBM antibodies. (2) Immunosuppression: IV pulse methylprednisolone (500-1000 mg daily x 3 days) followed by oral prednisone taper, PLUS cyclophosphamide (2 mg/kg/day for 2-3 months) to suppress new antibody formation. (3) Supportive care: blood transfusion for severe anemia, oxygen support, dialysis if needed for hyperkalemia/acidosis/volume overload. Renal prognosis is poor if presenting creatinine >5.7 mg/dL or dialysis dependence; transplantation can be considered after anti-GBM antibodies are undetectable for 6-12 months.
high_yield_pearls: Anti-GBM disease targets the NC1 domain of alpha-3(IV) collagen; the pulmonary-renal syndrome (Goodpasture syndrome) occurs when both alveolar and glomerular basement membranes are involved.
Linear IgG staining on immunofluorescence is PATHOGNOMONIC for anti-GBM disease (in contrast to granular staining in immune complex diseases).
Smoking and hydrocarbon exposure are risk factors for pulmonary involvement — they disrupt the alveolar capillary barrier, exposing the target antigen.
An elevated DLCO in a patient with hemoptysis is paradoxical and characteristic of diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (intra-alveolar blood absorbs CO).
Renal prognosis is primarily determined by the degree of crescent formation: patients with >80% crescents and dialysis dependence at presentation rarely recover renal function.
sources: McAdoo SP, Pusey CD. Anti-glomerular basement membrane disease. Clin J Am Soc Nephrol. 2017;12(7):1162-1172. — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=McAdoo+SP%2C+Pusey+CD.+Anti-glomerular+basement+membrane+disease.+Clin+J+Am+Soc+Nephrol.+2017%3B12%287%29%3A1162-1172.

Levy JB et al. Long-term outcome of anti-glomerular basement membrane antibody disease treated with plasma exchange and immunosuppression. Ann Intern Med. 2001;134(11):1033-1042. — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Levy+JB+et+al.+Long-term+outcome+of+anti-glomerular+basement+membrane+antibody+disease+treated+with+plasma+exchange+and+immunosuppression.+Ann+Intern+Med.+2001%3B134%2811%29%3A1033-1042.
UpToDate: Anti-GBM (Goodpasture) disease — pathogenesis, clinical manifestations, and diagnosis (2024) — https://www.uptodate.com/contents/search

after_stage2_more_likely: Anti-GBM disease — young male smoker with hydrocarbon exposure presenting with hemoptysis and rapidly progressive renal failure (pulmonary-renal syndrome)
ANCA vasculitis (GPA) — also causes pulmonary-renal syndrome, but typically has ENT involvement (sinusitis, nasal crusting)
after_stage2_less_likely: Lupus nephritis — young male, no rash, joint pain, or other systemic lupus features
Pulmonary embolism — does not explain nephritic sediment and rapidly rising creatinine
after_labs_top3: Anti-GBM disease — positive anti-GBM antibodies, linear IgG on biopsy, crescentic GN, ANCA negative
ANCA vasculitis — ANCA is negative; would show pauci-immune GN on biopsy, not linear IgG
Dual anti-GBM/ANCA disease — ANCA is negative in this case, ruling out dual positivity
